restoreSteps.step1: Tries to apply all migrations to head (the correct way)

restoreSteps.manualAlternative: Alternatively, you can manually apply migrations using the "To head" button in the migrations section above.

migrations.title: Alembic Migrations
migrations.notInitialized: Database not initialized. Click "To head" to apply all migrations.
migrations.latestDescription: The newest migration in the code
migrations.applyXNewMigrations: {{count}} migrations need to be applied. Click "To head" to update the database.
migrations.allApplied: All migrations applied. Database is up to date.
migrations.howToUseHead: "To head" — apply all unapplied migrations to the latest (recommended)
migrations.howToUseApply: "Apply" — apply a migration from the input field:
migrations.howToUseApplyHead: head — apply all to the latest
migrations.howToUseApplyPlusOne: +1 — apply the next single migration (if current is 1a99f4734fc4, the next one will be applied)
migrations.howToUseDowngradeBase: base — downgrade all migrations to the beginning
migrations.howToUseClickHint: Or click the up arrow next to a specific migration in the table
migrations.statusExplanationTitle: What do the migration statuses mean:
migrations.statusAppliedDesc: The migration is already applied to the database. Tables/columns have been created.
migrations.statusPendingDesc: The migration has not yet been applied. It needs to be applied to update the DB.
migrations.statusHeadDesc: The latest migration in the code. There are no other migrations after it.
migrations.currentLabelHint: Current — label indicates the current database revision (the last applied migration).

catalogPage.seoKeywords: filament catalog, 3D printing, PLA, PETG, ABS, print settings, slicer presets

catalogPage.heroSubtitle: Materials database with official manufacturer presets and community-verified settings

downloadPage.alertLoading: Build information is still loading. Please wait...
downloadPage.alertNotAvailable: Build is not yet available. It will be available for download soon!
downloadPage.headerSubtitle: The only version of OrcaSlicer with an integrated materials catalog
downloadPage.screenshotTabDesc: A new tab right in the main OrcaSlicer window — materials catalog, settings search, and preset sync without switching between applications.
downloadPage.screenshotCatalogDesc: Full-featured FilamentHub materials catalog right in the slicer — browse settings, filter by printers, and import presets instantly.
downloadPage.screenshotSyncDesc: Automatic sync of your presets between FilamentHub and OrcaSlicer — work on any device, settings are always up to date.
downloadPage.screenshotImportDesc: Found the right settings? Import a preset directly into OrcaSlicer with one click — no manual file copying or folder searching.
downloadPage.videoDesc: See how FilamentHub integration works in OrcaSlicer
downloadPage.benefitAllInOneDesc: No need to open a browser, search for settings, and manually copy files. Materials catalog, search, and import — right in OrcaSlicer.
downloadPage.benefitSyncDesc: Your presets sync between FilamentHub and OrcaSlicer automatically. Work on different computers — settings are always up to date.
downloadPage.benefitLibraryDesc: Access thousands of verified settings from the community and manufacturers. Filter by printer, material, and rating — find the perfect parameters.
downloadPage.benefitVerifiedDesc: Use settings with high ratings and success rates from other users. Save time on calibration — print with quality from the start.
downloadPage.benefitFullDesc: This is a full version of OrcaSlicer with all features. We only added FilamentHub integration — nothing was removed or changed in the slicer.
downloadPage.benefitFreeDesc: FilamentHub Edition is completely free and based on open source code. All changes are available in our repository — transparent and secure.
downloadPage.buildNotReady: {{platform}} build is not ready yet. Compilation requires the corresponding operating system.
downloadPage.buildFromSource: You can build the version yourself from source code or download from GitHub.
downloadPage.included1: Full version of OrcaSlicer with all features — nothing removed
downloadPage.included2: New "FilamentHub" tab in the main slicer window
downloadPage.included3: Built-in materials catalog with search and filters
downloadPage.included4: Automatic preset sync between FilamentHub and OrcaSlicer
downloadPage.included5: One-click settings import directly from the catalog
downloadPage.included6: Preset management from the slicer — create, edit, export
downloadPage.installWin2: Run the installer and follow the instructions
downloadPage.installWin3: After installation, open OrcaSlicer — the FilamentHub tab will be available in the main window
downloadPage.installWin4: Log in to your FilamentHub account via the FilamentHub tab
downloadPage.installMacNotice: macOS build is in development. Compilation requires macOS with Xcode.
downloadPage.installMacAfterRelease: After release, the instructions will be:
downloadPage.installMac2: Open the .dmg file and drag OrcaSlicer to the Applications folder
downloadPage.installMac3: On first launch, macOS may request permission — click "Open" in security settings
downloadPage.installMac4: Open OrcaSlicer — the FilamentHub tab will be available in the main window
downloadPage.installMac5: Log in to your FilamentHub account via the FilamentHub tab
downloadPage.installLinux4: Open OrcaSlicer — the FilamentHub tab will be available in the main window
downloadPage.installLinux5: Log in to your FilamentHub account via the FilamentHub tab
downloadPage.installLinuxNote: AppImage does not require installation — just download, make executable, and run. For system integration, you can use AppImageLauncher.
downloadPage.faq1Q: What's the difference between FilamentHub Edition and regular OrcaSlicer?
downloadPage.faq1A1_post: with an additional FilamentHub tab. All original slicer features are preserved — we only added convenient access to the materials catalog right in the interface.
downloadPage.faq1A2_text: No need to switch between browser and slicer, all settings are available in one window. Automatic preset sync saves time, and access to a library of verified settings helps you print with better quality from the start.
downloadPage.faq2Q: Do I need a FilamentHub account to use it?
downloadPage.faq2A_text: You can browse the materials catalog and import settings without registration. An account is only needed if you want to sync your presets between devices, save favorite materials, and share your settings with the community. Registration is free and takes less than a minute.
downloadPage.faq3Q: Can FilamentHub Edition be updated separately?
downloadPage.faq3A: Yes! FilamentHub Edition updates independently and includes all original OrcaSlicer updates plus integration improvements. We follow new OrcaSlicer versions and regularly release FilamentHub Edition updates. New version notifications appear right in the slicer.
downloadPage.faq4A1_text: FilamentHub Edition is based on OrcaSlicer's open source code. All our changes are available in an open repository — you can check every line of code.
downloadPage.faq4A2: We only add secure integration via WebView — no changes to the slicer core, no external dependencies, no questionable modifications. All communications with FilamentHub go through HTTPS, tokens are stored locally in encrypted form.
downloadPage.faq5Q: What if I already have regular OrcaSlicer installed?
downloadPage.faq5A: FilamentHub Edition can be installed alongside regular OrcaSlicer — they don't conflict. All your OrcaSlicer presets and settings will remain in place. FilamentHub Edition simply adds a tab with the materials catalog without affecting existing configuration. You can use both versions simultaneously!
downloadPage.faq6Q: Does it work on all platforms?
downloadPage.faq6A1_post: (Intel and Apple Silicon) build will come later.
downloadPage.faq6A2_text: Windows (installer + portable) and Linux (AppImage) are ready to use. macOS will come later (building requires the corresponding system). You can also build a version for your platform yourself from source code — instructions are in the repository.

spoolHelp.title: My Filaments — how it works
spoolHelp.line1: Add a spool manually or via QR code — the system will match the material from the catalog.
spoolHelp.line2: Spool price is calculated automatically: price per kg × initial weight.
spoolHelp.line3: If you have Happy Hare MMU configured — after adding a spool you can immediately assign it to a gate slot.
spoolHelp.line4: Gate assignment is done from the web UI; Happy Hare sees it via the Spoolman-compatible API.

help.title: How to use your profile
help.presetsDesc: Presets are complete print settings for a specific filament: nozzle and bed temperatures, speeds, retraction, cooling, pressure advance, and much more. Each preset is linked to a specific material from the catalog and can be synced with OrcaSlicer.
help.presetsStep1: Click "New preset" on the "Presets" tab — the creation form will open
help.presetsStep2: Select a filament from the catalog. If it's missing — create a new one via the "+" button (enter brand, name, type, color)
help.presetsStep3: Fill in the main parameters: nozzle temperature, bed temperature, print speed, retraction. For PLA typically: nozzle 200–215°C, bed 55–65°C
help.presetsStep4: For fine-tuning, expand "Detailed settings" — tabs mirror OrcaSlicer: speeds, cooling, pressure/PA, overhang, etc.
help.presetsNote: Presets can be edited, duplicated, and deleted. You can also save other users' presets from the catalog via the "To profile" button.
help.printersDesc: Manage your 3D printers. Each printer contains printer profiles (bed dimensions, kinematics, G-code) and print profiles (quality: 0.08–0.32 mm).
help.printersStep1: Click "Create printer profile" — specify the model, bed dimensions, kinematics type, nozzle diameters
help.printersStep2: Add print profiles (Draft 0.3, Quality 0.2, Fine 0.12, etc.) — these define layer height and other quality parameters
help.printersStep3: If your printer isn't in the list — click "Request printer" and an admin will add it to the database
help.printersNote: Printer profiles sync with OrcaSlicer just like filament presets.
help.calculatorDesc: A tool for calculating 3D printing costs. Accounts for all key expenses: material, electricity, printer depreciation, work time, and your markup.
help.calculatorStep1: Select a filament and enter model weight (in grams) — material cost will be calculated automatically
help.calculatorStep2: Set print time, electricity rate, and printer cost for depreciation calculation
help.calculatorStep3: Add a markup (%) — the calculator will show the final price for the customer
help.syncDesc: FilamentHub is integrated with a modified OrcaSlicer. All your presets and printer profiles automatically appear in the slicer — no need to manually transfer settings.
help.syncStep1: Go to "Settings" (gear icon) and enable the "OrcaSlicer Sync" toggle
help.syncStep2: On each preset, click the sync icon (🔄) to make it available in the slicer
help.syncStep3: Open OrcaSlicer (FilamentHub version) — your presets will already be in the materials list
help.syncNote: Sync works both ways: changes in OrcaSlicer will also reflect on the website.
help.brandDesc: If you represent a filament brand, you can get a verified manufacturer account. This grants access to create official presets on behalf of the brand, which will be marked as recommended.
help.brandStep1: Switch to "Company Profile" at the top of the page
help.brandStep2: Fill in your company information and submit a verification request
help.brandStep3: Once approved by a moderator, you'll be able to add filaments and presets on behalf of the brand
help.settingsDesc: In settings you can manage your account, sync, and display preferences:
help.settingsStep1: Change your username, email, password, and avatar
help.hhDesc: Happy Hare is firmware for MMU systems (ERCF, Box Turtle, etc.). FilamentHub integrates via the Spoolman protocol: assign spools to gates on the website, and the printer picks up changes automatically.
help.hhConnectStep1: Find spoolman_support and set it to: pull
help.hhConnectStep2: Find t_macro_color and set it to: gatemap
help.hhConnectStep3: Set your FilamentHub URL as the Spoolman endpoint (API key — in Profile Settings → Sync)
help.hhSpoolmanReadonly: readonly — HH reads spool attributes, gate assignments not synced
help.hhSpoolmanPush: push — HH writes gate map to FilamentHub, but does not read changes from the site
help.hhSpoolmanPull: pull ✓ — bidirectional: site assignments are applied on the printer (recommended)
help.hhColorSlicer: slicer — color from G-code file (default, ignores FilamentHub)
help.hhColorGatemap: gatemap ✓ — real spool colors from FilamentHub, empty gates hidden (recommended)
help.hhColorAllgates: allgates — colors for all gates including empty ones
help.hhWorkflow: Workflow: buy spool → scan QR → assign gate in "My Filaments" → printer in Mainsail shows the correct color and material automatically
help.tip1: Save presets you like from other users via the "To profile" button in the catalog — they'll appear in your list
help.tip2: Enable sync on each preset (🔄) to make it available in OrcaSlicer
help.tip3: Use material type (PLA, PETG, ABS...) and brand filters in the catalog
help.tip4: Click on a preset card to view all settings in a modal — you can also download JSON from there
help.tip5: Use the "Dashboard" tab for a quick overview: recent presets, statistics, activity
help.premiumCalculatorDesc: Advanced calculator with G-code upload, calculation history, quote generation, and automatic print parameter parsing

profilePage.calculatorSubtitleShort: Calculate the cost of a part
profilePage.calculatorSubtitleLong: Calculate the exact cost of a part with regional adjustments
profilePage.myPrintersDescription: Manage printers and their profiles. Sync with OrcaSlicer.

calc.taxRateHint: 0% = no tax, 4-6% = local self-employment / simplified tax
calc.partsPerPrintHint: How many parts fit into one print run. Affects the total machine time for the batch.
calc.minOrderPriceHint: If price is lower, minimum is applied (usually 300-500 ₽)
calc.taxAmountHint: Tax is included in the final price and is not treated as profit
calc.profitMarginHint: Profit = price before tax - cost of goods

calculator.title: Print Cost Calculator
calculator.subtitle: Professional cost breakdown for 3D printing jobs with all major expenses included
calculator.methodHint: Choose the estimate model that matches the job: quick material math, time-based pricing, or the full combined breakdown with overhead and adjustment coefficients.
calculator.gcodeDropDescription: Upload a ready G-code file to parse print time, weight, preview, and auto-fill the estimate form.
calculator.gcodeParsedDescription: Calculator Pro extracted the print metrics and applied them to the estimate form.
calculator.previewUnavailable: Preview is unavailable for this file.
calculator.gcodeSoonTitle: G-code parsing is the next phase
calculator.gcodeSoonDescription: Calculator Pro already runs on the real backend estimate engine. G-code ingestion stays as the next roadmap step instead of a fake placeholder flow.
calculator.resultsEmptyTitle: The right panel comes alive after the first estimate
calculator.resultsEmptyDescription: Fill the parameters on the left and run the estimate. This panel will show the cost breakdown, final price, and the key job metrics.
calculator.historySaveError: Failed to save the estimate to history
calculator.historyDeleteError: Failed to remove the estimate from history
calculator.noHistoryDescription: Save the first estimate and it will appear here
calculator.name: Name
calculator.staticSettingsDescription: This panel stores local defaults for both calculation economics and commercial offer details.
calculator.staticEconomicsDescription: Persistent rates and thresholds that should auto-fill every new estimate.
calculator.staticEconomicsNote: These are starter defaults for a small workshop, not authoritative pricing rules. Adjust them to your real rates, tax setup, and equipment fleet.
calculator.defaultElectricityCostTooltip: A starting point for a home or small commercial electricity tariff. If you print in a workshop or shared space, it is better to use your actual kWh price.
calculator.defaultPrinterPowerTooltip: A rough average power draw for an FDM printer during operation. Enclosed machines, large beds, and extra heaters can consume more.
calculator.defaultPrintingRateTooltip: A baseline machine-hour rate for a small FDM operation. Treat it as a starting point, not as a recommended market price.
calculator.defaultModelingRateTooltip: This rate is higher than printing because it includes skill, model preparation, revisions, and customer communication. If you do not offer modeling, set it to zero.
calculator.defaultPostprocessingRateTooltip: A starter rate for manual work after printing: removing supports, cleanup, basic finishing, and packing.
calculator.defaultAmortizationRateTooltip: A rough hourly depreciation value for a desktop printer. Recalculate it from your equipment cost, expected lifetime, and repair budget.
calculator.defaultOverheadTooltip: Indirect costs on top of direct production: rent, consumables, services, admin overhead, and other recurring expenses.
calculator.defaultMarkupTooltip: The commercial markup above cost and overhead. This is not tax and not pure margin by itself; it is the lever that shapes your market-facing price.
calculator.workspaceDescription: Calculator Pro assembles the estimate like a commercial offer: order data first, then production, then rare adjustments and the final batch result.
calculator.workspaceSourceDescription: Upload G-code for exact source data or keep the manual scenario and fill in the estimate yourself.
calculator.workspaceSourceHint: G-code speeds up quoting, but it is optional
calculator.workspaceMaterialDescription: Choose the material and confirm the spool parameters that affect the production cost.
calculator.workspaceProductionDescription: Set the batch size and the real machine time for one print run.
calculator.workspaceBatchHint: Batch math uses the number of print runs, not raw quantity
calculator.workspaceTimeHint: When G-code is loaded, time usually needs only a correction
calculator.workspaceGcodeSummaryDescription: These values came from the file and help verify that the quote is based on the correct source.
calculator.coreInputsDescription: Keep only what usually changes from quote to quote here: material, weight, time, and batch parameters.
calculator.advancedInputsDescription: Less frequent adjustments for supports, delivery, extra work, and pricing coefficients.
calculator.advancedInputsSummary: Supports, delivery, modeling, post-processing, and correction coefficients.
calculator.quoteProfileDescription: Default executor details, quote validity, legal status, and payment terms. In the quote modal they can be overridden for a single document without changing the saved profile.
calculator.quoteProfileSummaryEmpty: Executor details are not filled in yet
calculator.quoteBuilderDescription: Review executor and customer details, then open the printable version to save it as PDF or send it to the client.
calculator.quoteSellerDescription: These fields are prefilled from the saved quote profile. You can override them for the current document only.
calculator.quoteValidityDaysHint: Default validity period from the document issue date.
calculator.quoteDisclaimerHint: This line is printed in the commercial offer document.
calculator.quoteDisclaimerNotOffer: This document is not a public offer
calculator.quoteDisclaimerOffer: This document is a public offer
calculator.quotePaymentTermsPlaceholder: For example: 100% prepayment, payment due within 3 business days
calculator.quoteBuyerDescription: These fields appear in the document header.
calculator.quoteChecklistLineItems: Quoted item with quantity and per-unit price
calculator.quoteChecklistCosts: Breakdown for material, labor, electricity, and depreciation
calculator.quoteChecklistPrint: Printable version ready for Save as PDF in the browser
calculator.quotePopupBlocked: The browser blocked the print window. Allow pop-ups for FilamentHub.

quoteIncluded.none: The included service breakdown is not available yet

calculator.quoteDocumentSubtitle: 3D printing cost estimate and related services
calculator.quoteTaxStatus: Pricing is based on FilamentHub internal costing. Tax is included in the total when a rate is set.

flags.relativeEDesc: Enables relative extrusion mode in G-code (M83).
flags.pelletModDesc: Printer is modified for pellets / pellet extruder.
flags.multiBedTypesDesc: Supports different plates and bed profiles.

cooling.speedupOverhangsDesc: Orca stores this as a boolean flag, not as a percent.

motion.emitMachineLimitsDesc: Writes machine limit commands into generated G-code when the firmware profile expects them.
motion.resonanceAvoidanceDesc: Matches Orca's QIDI-derived ringing mitigation by slowing outer walls inside the resonance speed band.

extruder.variantsHint: One line per extruder. Each line may contain comma-separated supported variants for that extruder.
extruder.amsCountHint: One line per extruder. Orca uses AMS pairs like 0#1|1#1.
extruder.commonArchitectures: Vendor docs commonly reference direct drive and Bowden layouts. Bondtech BMG supports both; Micro Swiss NG is a geared direct-drive example; Dyze Pulsar represents pellet extrusion. Keep stored Orca enum values compatible: Direct Drive or Bowden.
extruder.commonNozzleMaterials: Vendor docs commonly reference brass, hardened steel, stainless steel, tungsten carbide, plus more specialized constructions like vanadium, ruby-tip, and diamond-tip nozzles. Keep Orca-compatible enum values in metadata and record finer vendor details in variants or notes.
extruder.commonNozzleFlowFamilies: Flow families are typically Standard vs High Flow. Common vendor examples include Prusa CHT and E3D/Revo High Flow style nozzles.

retraction.restartExtraToolchange: Extra length on restart after toolchange (mm)
retraction.longRetractionOnCutDesc: Controls whether long retraction while cutting is configured at machine level or deferred to filament overrides.
retraction.boolArrayHint: Use 0/1 per extruder, one value per line.

multi.machineToolChangeTimeHint: Uses the canonical Orca key machine_tool_change_time. Legacy machine_switch_extruder_time is normalized on save.

help.oneValuePerLine: One value per line. This preserves arrays whose items may include commas.

tooltips.junctionDeviation: Controls how much paths are rounded through corners. Lower values hold corners more accurately but can slow the printer more; higher values smooth turns more aggressively and may increase ringing.
tooltips.singleExtruderMultiMaterial: SEMM stands for Single Extruder Multi Material. Use it when multiple materials or colors share one hotend and are fed through it one after another instead of using separate nozzles.
tooltips.bedTemperatureFormula: Chooses which loaded filament drives the bed temperature in multi-material prints: the first filament or the one with the highest bed-temperature requirement.
tooltips.wipeTower: A wipe tower is a sacrificial structure printed next to the part. It receives purge material during color or material changes so the previous material does not contaminate the model.
tooltips.purgeInPrimeTower: When enabled, purge and cleaning moves during material changes are sent into the wipe tower instead of the model or open air.
tooltips.ramming: Ramming shapes the filament tip before unloading by compressing molten material in a controlled way. This reduces jams and stringy tips during material changes.
tooltips.semm: This block tunes loading and unloading behavior for SEMM workflows: how far to retract, where to stop the filament in the cooling tube, and how much extra push to apply on reload.
tooltips.parkingRetraction: How far the filament retracts before parking or switching. This helps release pressure in the hotend before unloading or waiting.
tooltips.coolingTubeRetraction: The target filament position inside the cooling tube before the rest of the unload sequence continues. It strongly affects how repeatable the filament tip shape is.
tooltips.coolingTubeLength: The effective cooling tube length in the unload path. If this is wrong, the slicer will mistime where the tip is formed.
tooltips.extraLoadingMove: An extra push after the main load move. Useful when the filament needs a little more travel to reliably reach the melt zone.
tooltips.toolChangeTime: A slicer-side estimate for how long a tool or material change takes. It affects time calculation and planning, not the printer mechanics directly.

nozzleGuide.title: Nozzle material guide
nozzleGuide.caption: Use this as a selection aid. Final temperatures and speed still depend on the hotend, the specific filament, and the vendor profile for that material.

brass.temperature: Baseline reference for PLA, PETG, and other non-abrasive materials. Usually does not need extra temperature versus the profile.
brass.speed: Best everyday heat transfer balance for standard print speeds and typical flow.

hardened_steel.temperature: Often needs a small bump compared with brass because thermal transfer is lower.
hardened_steel.speed: Strong all-round choice, but at very high speeds it usually carries less heat than copper or carbide.

stainless_steel.temperature: Usually works with a slight increase over brass temperatures, especially on more viscous materials.
stainless_steel.speed: Better kept at moderate speeds; not the strongest option for aggressive high-flow setups.

tungsten_carbide.temperature: Typically stays stable across the working range and keeps flow consistent even on hotter engineering materials.
tungsten_carbide.speed: Well suited to faster printing and higher flow if the rest of the extrusion path can keep up.

copper_alloy.temperature: High thermal transfer helps with hotter materials, but the exact window still depends on coating and nozzle construction.
copper_alloy.speed: Works well for increased flow as long as the nozzle is not a soft copper design intended only for non-abrasive use.

ruby.temperature: Usually stable across normal printing temperatures, but the nozzle should not be stressed by crashes or misalignment.
ruby.speed: Normal to moderately high speeds are fine when the machine is well aligned and mechanically stable.

titanium.temperature: Lower thermal transfer can require a higher setpoint or a more conservative material profile.
titanium.speed: Usually better for calmer print speeds and controlled flow than for chasing maximum throughput.

nickel_plated_copper.temperature: Handles hotter materials well and often supports more stable feeding near the upper end of the temperature range.
nickel_plated_copper.speed: A good fit for higher-flow printing when the specific model has enough wear resistance for the job.

E3D.temperature: This is an Orca vendor bucket rather than a single material. Check the actual nozzle material in the spec for that model.
E3D.speed: Speed behavior depends on the exact E3D nozzle family: standard, hardened, ObXidian, High Flow, and so on.

gcode.legacyCompatibilityHint: These fields remain editable for backward compatibility with existing FilamentHub data even though they are not part of the canonical Orca printer config page.

badgeDesc.contributor: Contributor. User who made a significant contribution to the project: code, documentation, translations, design, or other useful materials.
badgeDesc.verified: Verified manufacturer. Official representative of a 3D printing materials brand who confirmed their affiliation with the company.
badgeDesc.early_adopter: Early adopter. User who started using FilamentHub in the first months after launch and actively participates in the community.
badgeDesc.supporter: Project supporter. User who financially or otherwise supported the development of FilamentHub.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Report bugs, suggest features, or ask a question
feedback.sendError: Failed to send message. Please try again later.
feedback.messageSent: Your message has been sent. We will review it shortly.

bug.label: Bug
bug.subjectPlaceholder: Example: "Error when creating a preset on the filament page"
bug.messagePlaceholder:
Describe the issue in detail:

1. What were you doing before the error occurred? (steps to reproduce)
2. What behavior did you expect?
3. What actually happened?
4. On which page/section did the error occur?
5. Are you using a browser on mobile or desktop?
6. Do you have screenshots or error messages?

The more detail you provide, the faster we can fix it!
bug.instructions: For bugs, it is important to describe the steps to reproduce and expected behavior

feature.label: Suggestion
feature.subjectPlaceholder: Example: "Add a filter by material rating"
feature.messagePlaceholder:
Describe your suggestion:

1. What feature would you like to see?
2. How would it be useful?
3. How do you envision it?
4. Are there examples from other services that could serve as inspiration?

Your ideas help make FilamentHub better!
feature.instructions: Describe the feature, its purpose, and how it should work

question.label: Question
question.subjectPlaceholder: Example: "How to sync presets with OrcaSlicer?"
question.messagePlaceholder:
Ask your question:

Describe what you are interested in or what you need help with. We will try to answer as soon as possible!
question.instructions: Ask your question and we will definitely respond

other.label: Other
other.messagePlaceholder:
Describe your question, suggestion, or issue in detail.

Your feedback is very important to us!
